game_name: Dungeons & Dragons: Warriors of the Eternal Sun

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial-like initial encounters establish turn-based combat rhythm
Clerics introduced for healing support; thieves for trap disarming; magic-users for range
Introduces dungeon mode with real-time combat, different spell effects (lightning bounce)
Artifacts gate progression - required to access deeper areas (swamp, caves)
Provides knowledge enabling access to northern caves
Yields items necessary for volcano exploration
Escalates tension; townspeople increasingly insane as party adventures
Requires ancient spell summoning Ka the Preserver to defeat

typical_tension_triggers: Real-time combat in dungeon mode (contrasts with turn-based exterior)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical escalation: exterior turn-based exploration punctuated by dungeon real-time encounters; tension builds through discovery of madness plaguing castle; final spike at antagonist revelation

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Artifact discovery gates progression; knowledge from hostile factions unlocks new areas
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Dungeon mode uses first-person view with compass and textual descriptions (mimics tabletop DM narration); hidden knowledge available through hostage/conquered factions

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory combat encounters with goblins/beastmen; character creation establishes baseline ability scores (max 18); turn-based exterior mode allows tactical positioning
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Hazard escalation: dungeon real-time combat contrasts with exterior turn-based; hostile factions require combat to gain knowledge; artifact requirement gates access to swamp and caves
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sanity mechanic escalates: NPCs progressively turn hostile; final dungeons (volcano) required to reach ally; revelation of Burrower and Thanatos as hidden orchestrators
notable_spikes: Transition to first-person dungeon mode (speed/perspective shift)
Discovery that castle NPCs have gone mad
Final antagonist revelation requiring ancient spell acquisition

notable_level_patterns.description: Isometric world-map exploration in turn-based mode transitions to first-person real-time dungeon encounters; compass and textual descriptions provide spatial orientation; weapons/spells have dungeon-specific effects
Hostile factions (beastmen, lizardmen, Azcans) must be defeated to acquire knowledge/artifacts unlocking subsequent areas; creates narrative tension between combat necessity and lore discovery
Items collected from defeated factions enable exploration of new regions; castle return serves as narrative inflection point where madness becomes apparent
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Beastman cave artifacts → swamp/cave access; Azcan temple items → volcano access

notable_level_patterns.description: Off-screen NPC madness accumulates while party adventures; payoff occurs upon castle return, shifting threat from external (monsters) to internal (corrupted allies)
